message.reasoning:
We need to follow instructions: respond with JSON only, matching the response contract.

User wants to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions, focusing on locating schema version constants. The selected history shows previous attempts: expand_file, hydrate_source, etc. The graph frontier suggests "expand_file" as recommended action. Probably next step is to expand_file on crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs to get more symbols.

We need to produce a JSON with kind: tool_call, tool_name: "session.expand_file@v1", payload with target selector. Which target? Likely the file path: "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Also maybe include depth etc. Use short ids? The rules: Use exact short ids as provided in the prompt when referring to graph nodes. Short ids look like F1, S3, D2, or R1. In the graph relations we have many symbols with short id like [S1], [S2], etc. But we need to refer to graph nodes using short ids? The instruction: "Use exact short ids as provided in the prompt when referring to graph nodes." The prompt includes short ids? In the selected history and graph frontier they use bracketed identifiers like [F1] etc. Those are short ids? They are labelled like [F1] for file? Might be node categories. But I think we can refer to the file via its logical_key maybe "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". However the rule says use exact short ids as provided in the prompt when referring to graph nodes. The prompt includes short ids like "F1"? At the top they say short ids look like F1, S3, D2, or R1. In the graph relations they use bracketed ids like [F1] etc. So maybe we need to refer to the file node as something like "F1"? But we need to be more precise: The file is the root node, maybe its short id is "F1"? In the graph frontier they say "Focus: crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs | visible_nodes=38 ..." So maybe the short id for that file is "F1"? Actually earlier they used "F1" as a label for file in graph relations: "[F1] crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs --exports--> [S1] symbol:...". So "F1" likely refers to the file node. So we can refer to it using short id "F1". The tool session.expand_file expects a target selector; perhaps we can give "F1". So payload: {"target":"F1"} maybe.

Thus we need to output JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"<brief status>",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1","payload":{...}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

But we need to fill assistant_message with brief status. Probably something like "Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs". Use short string.

Also "acquired_fact_ids" likely empty until we get evidence. So can be [].

Readiness_state: maybe "not_ready" because we haven't got evidence yet.

Make sure JSON is valid, no extra fields.

Thus produce:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target":"F1"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Check schema: response contract says: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. So acquired_fact_ids is an array of strings; we can provide empty array [].

Thus final JSON.
